Capabilities.description: The supported authentication types, data interface types (compute environments), and data operations of the connector.

ConnectionProperties.description: Connection properties which are common across compute environments.

ConnectionOptions.description: Returns properties that can be set when creating a connection in the ConnectionInput.ConnectionProperties. ConnectionOptions defines parameters that can be set in a Spark ETL script in the connection options map passed to a dataframe.

AuthenticationConfiguration.description: The type of authentication used for the connection.

ComputeEnvironmentConfigurations.description: The compute environments that are supported by the connection.

PhysicalConnectionRequirements.description: Physical requirements for a connection, such as VPC, Subnet and Security Group specifications.

AthenaConnectionProperties.description: Connection properties specific to the Athena compute environment.

PythonConnectionProperties.description: Connection properties specific to the Python compute environment.

SparkConnectionProperties.description: Connection properties specific to the Spark compute environment.

Fields.description: Describes the fields for that connector entity. This is the list of Field objects. Field is very similar to column in a database. The Field object has information about different properties associated with fields in the connector.

NextToken.description: A continuation token, present if the current segment is not the last.
A continuation token, if not all blueprint runs have been returned.

Catalog.description: A Catalog object. The definition of the specified catalog in the Glue Data Catalog.

ColumnStatisticsList.description: List of ColumnStatistics that failed to be retrieved.

Errors.description: Error occurred during retrieving column statistics data.
List of ColumnStatistics that failed to be retrieved.

ColumnStatisticsTaskRun.description: A ColumnStatisticsTaskRun object representing the details of the column stats run.

ColumnStatisticsTaskRuns.description: A list of column statistics task runs.

NextToken.description: A continuation token, if not all task runs have yet been returned.

ColumnStatisticsTaskSettings.description: A ColumnStatisticsTaskSettings object representing the settings for the column statistics task.

Name.description: The name of the custom pattern that you retrieved.

RegexString.description: A regular expression string that is used for detecting sensitive data in a custom pattern.

ContextWords.description: A list of context words if specified when you created the custom pattern. If none of these context words are found within the vicinity of the regular expression the data will not be detected as sensitive data.

Status.description: The training status of the data quality model.

StartedOn.description: The timestamp when the data quality model training started.

CompletedOn.description: The timestamp when the data quality model training completed.
The timestamp when the data quality model training completed.

ResultId.description: A unique result ID for the data quality result.

ProfileId.description: The Profile ID for the data quality result.

Score.description: An aggregate data quality score. Represents the ratio of rules that passed to the total number of rules.

DataSource.description: The table associated with the data quality result, if any.

RulesetName.description: The name of the ruleset associated with the data quality result.

EvaluationContext.description: In the context of a job in Glue Studio, each node in the canvas is typically assigned some sort of name and data quality nodes will have names. In the case of multiple nodes, the evaluationContext can differentiate the nodes.

StartedOn.description: The date and time when the run for this data quality result started.

CompletedOn.description: The date and time when the run for this data quality result was completed.

JobName.description: The job name associated with the data quality result, if any.

JobRunId.description: The job run ID associated with the data quality result, if any.

RulesetEvaluationRunId.description: The unique run ID associated with the ruleset evaluation.

RuleResults.description: A list of DataQualityRuleResult objects representing the results for each rule.

AnalyzerResults.description: A list of DataQualityAnalyzerResult objects representing the results for each analyzer.

Observations.description: A list of DataQualityObservation objects representing the observations generated after evaluating the rules and analyzers.

AggregatedMetrics.description: A summary of DataQualityAggregatedMetrics objects showing the total counts of processed rows and rules, including their pass/fail statistics based on row-level results.

RunId.description: The unique run identifier associated with this run.

DataSource.description: The data source (an Glue table) associated with this run.

Role.description: An IAM role supplied to encrypt the results of the run.

NumberOfWorkers.description: The number of G.1X workers to be used in the run. The default is 5.

Timeout.description: The timeout for a run in minutes. This is the maximum time that a run can consume resources before it is terminated and enters TIMEOUT status. The default is 2,880 minutes (48 hours).

ErrorString.description: The error strings that are associated with the run.

StartedOn.description: The date and time when this run started.

LastModifiedOn.description: A timestamp. The last point in time when this data quality rule recommendation run was modified.

CompletedOn.description: The date and time when this run was completed.

ExecutionTime.description: The amount of time (in seconds) that the run consumed resources.

RecommendedRuleset.description: When a start rule recommendation run completes, it creates a recommended ruleset (a set of rules). This member has those rules in Data Quality Definition Language (DQDL) format.

CreatedRulesetName.description: The name of the ruleset that was created by the run.

DataQualitySecurityConfiguration.description: The name of the security configuration created with the data quality encryption option.

Ruleset.description: A Data Quality Definition Language (DQDL) ruleset. For more information, see the Glue developer guide.

TargetTable.description: The name and database name of the target table.

CreatedOn.description: A timestamp. The time and date that this data quality ruleset was created.

LastModifiedOn.description: A timestamp. The last point in time when this data quality ruleset was modified.

RecommendationRunId.description: When a ruleset was created from a recommendation run, this run ID is generated to link the two together.

DataQualitySecurityConfiguration.description: The name of the security configuration created with the data quality encryption option.

RunId.description: The unique run identifier associated with this run.

DataSource.description: The data source (an Glue table) associated with this evaluation run.

Role.description: An IAM role supplied to encrypt the results of the run.

NumberOfWorkers.description: The number of G.1X workers to be used in the run. The default is 5.

Timeout.description: The timeout for a run in minutes. This is the maximum time that a run can consume resources before it is terminated and enters TIMEOUT status. The default is 2,880 minutes (48 hours).

AdditionalRunOptions.description: Additional run options you can specify for an evaluation run.

ErrorString.description: The error strings that are associated with the run.

StartedOn.description: The date and time when this run started.

LastModifiedOn.description: A timestamp. The last point in time when this data quality rule recommendation run was modified.

CompletedOn.description: The date and time when this run was completed.

ExecutionTime.description: The amount of time (in seconds) that the run consumed resources.

RulesetNames.description: A list of ruleset names for the run. Currently, this parameter takes only one Ruleset name.

ResultIds.description: A list of result IDs for the data quality results for the run.

AdditionalDataSources.description: A map of reference strings to additional data sources you can specify for an evaluation run.

Database.description: The definition of the specified database in the Data Catalog.

NextToken.description: A continuation token, present if the current segment is not the last.

ResourceArn.description: The connection ARN of the source, or the database ARN of the target.

ResourcePropertyArn.description: The resource ARN created through this create API. The format is something like arn:aws:glue:<region>:<account_id>:integrationresourceproperty/*

SourceProcessingProperties.description: The resource properties associated with the integration source.

TargetProcessingProperties.description: The resource properties associated with the integration target.

ResourceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the target table for which to retrieve integration table properties. Currently, this API only supports retrieving properties for target tables, and the provided ARN should be the ARN of the target table in the Glue Data Catalog. Support for retrieving integration table properties for source connections (using the connection ARN) is not yet implemented and will be added in a future release.

TableName.description: The name of the table to be replicated.

SourceTableConfig.description: A structure for the source table configuration.

TargetTableConfig.description: A structure for the target table configuration.

JobBookmarkEntry.description: A structure that defines a point that a job can resume processing.

TransformId.description: The unique identifier of the task run.

TaskRunId.description: The unique run identifier associated with this run.

LogGroupName.description: The names of the log groups that are associated with the task run.

Properties.description: The list of properties that are associated with the task run.

ErrorString.description: The error strings that are associated with the task run.

StartedOn.description: The date and time when this task run started.

LastModifiedOn.description: The date and time when this task run was last modified.

CompletedOn.description: The date and time when this task run was completed.

ExecutionTime.description: The amount of time (in seconds) that the task run consumed resources.

TaskRuns.description: A list of task runs that are associated with the transform.

NextToken.description: A pagination token, if more results are available.

TransformId.description: The unique identifier of the transform, generated at the time that the transform was created.

Name.description: The unique name given to the transform when it was created.

Status.description: The last known status of the transform (to indicate whether it can be used or not). One of "NOT_READY", "READY", or "DELETING".

CreatedOn.description: The date and time when the transform was created.

LastModifiedOn.description: The date and time when the transform was last modified.

InputRecordTables.description: A list of Glue table definitions used by the transform.

Parameters.description: The configuration parameters that are specific to the algorithm used.

LabelCount.description: The number of labels available for this transform.

Schema.description: The Map<Column, Type> object that represents the schema that this transform accepts. Has an upper bound of 100 columns.

Role.description: The name or Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role with the required permissions.

GlueVersion.description: This value determines which version of Glue this machine learning transform is compatible with. Glue 1.0 is recommended for most customers. If the value is not set, the Glue compatibility defaults to Glue 0.9. For more information, see Glue Versions in the developer guide.

MaxCapacity.description:
The number of Glue data processing units (DPUs) that are allocated to task runs for this transform. You can allocate from 2 to 100 DPUs; the default is 10. A DPU is a relative measure of processing power that consists of 4 vCPUs of compute capacity and 16 GB of memory. For more information, see the Glue pricing page.

When the WorkerType field is set to a value other than Standard, the MaxCapacity field is set automatically and becomes read-only.

WorkerType.description:
The type of predefined worker that is allocated when this task runs. Accepts a value of Standard, G.1X, or G.2X.

  • For the Standard worker type, each worker provides 4 vCPU, 16 GB of memory and a 50GB disk, and 2 executors per worker.

  • For the G.1X worker type, each worker provides 4 vCPU, 16 GB of memory and a 64GB disk, and 1 executor per worker.

  • For the G.2X worker type, each worker provides 8 vCPU, 32 GB of memory and a 128GB disk, and 1 executor per worker.

NumberOfWorkers.description: The number of workers of a defined workerType that are allocated when this task runs.

Timeout.description: The timeout for a task run for this transform in minutes. This is the maximum time that a task run for this transform can consume resources before it is terminated and enters TIMEOUT status. The default is 2,880 minutes (48 hours).

MaxRetries.description: The maximum number of times to retry a task for this transform after a task run fails.

TransformEncryption.description: The encryption-at-rest settings of the transform that apply to accessing user data. Machine learning transforms can access user data encrypted in Amazon S3 using KMS.

Mapping.description: A list of mappings to the specified targets.

Partition.description: The requested information, in the form of a Partition object.

A continuation token, if the returned list of partitions does not include the last one.

PythonScript.description: A Python script to perform the mapping.

ScalaCode.description: The Scala code to perform the mapping.

CreatedTime.description: The date and time the registry was created.

UpdatedTime.description: The date and time the registry was updated.

RegistryArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the registry.

QueryAuthorizationId.description: A cryptographically generated query identifier generated by Glue or Lake Formation.

IsMultiDialectView.description: Specifies whether the view supports the SQL dialects of one or more different query engines and can therefore be read by those engines.

ResourceArn.description: The resource ARN of the parent resource extracted from the request.

IsProtected.description: A flag that instructs the engine not to push user-provided operations into the logical plan of the view during query planning. However, if set this flag does not guarantee that the engine will comply. Refer to the engine's documentation to understand the guarantees provided, if any.

Permissions.description: The Lake Formation data permissions of the caller on the table. Used to authorize the call when no view context is found.

RowFilter.description: The filter that applies to the table. For example when applying the filter in SQL, it would go in the WHERE clause and can be evaluated by using an AND operator with any other predicates applied by the user querying the table.

Configuration.description: A ProfileConfiguration object specifying the job and session values for the profile.

CreatedOn.description: The date and time when the usage profile was created.

LastModifiedOn.description: The date and time when the usage profile was last modified.
